meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve Can't Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your code. Learn to master portfolio building with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch high-impact projects that define your career.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' syndrome stems from scope creep and lack of modularity. To solve this, focus on building vertical slices—functional, deployable features that solve one specific problem. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you transform endless drafts into a high-impact, professional portfolio.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is where potential goes to die. You know the feeling: the initial spark of an idea, the feverish first night of coding, and then the slow, painful drift into technical debt and feature fatigue. It’s not a lack of talent holding you back; it’s a lack of a structured system for shipping.

Today, we stop the cycle. We are moving from 'tinkering' to 'architecting.' By treating your projects as professional products rather than homework assignments, you will build a portfolio that doesn't just show off code, but demonstrates the ability to execute, iterate, and deliver under pressure.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP
steps.description: Narrow your scope to a single, high-value feature. If it takes more than 72 hours to build, it is too big.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and strip it down to the absolute minimum viable feature set that provides standalone value. Identify the core user flow and remove all 'nice-to-have' features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --prompt 'Create a project roadmap focusing exclusively on the core MVP feature identified.'

steps.title: Architect with Antigravity
steps.description: Establish a clean, scalable folder structure before writing a single line of business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready directory structure for this project using a clean architecture pattern. Explain the purpose of each directory.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Generate the directory structure and boilerplate config files based on the Antigravity architecture schema.'

steps.title: Build the Vertical Slice
steps.description: Implement the full stack for one specific feature, from database schema to UI, before moving to the next.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the data schema and API endpoint for the primary user flow. Ensure error handling is robust and follows RESTful best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Implement the authentication and data persistence layer for the core user flow.'

steps.title: Automate Documentation
steps.description: Use your dev tools to generate documentation as you build, ensuring your portfolio shows process, not just code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a README.md that highlights the technical challenges faced, the architectural decisions made, and the specific problem solved by this feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --doc 'Generate documentation for current codebase including setup instructions and API overview.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: A project isn't finished until it is live. Deploy your MVP to a public URL to lock in the milestone.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a deployment checklist for this specific stack and identify the most efficient free-tier hosting options for this architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --deploy 'Configure deployment pipeline and provide status report of live environment.'

pro_tips: Never build in a vacuum; always write your README before the code to clarify your objectives.
Use 'Git-Flow' strictly to isolate features; if a feature breaks, you can revert without losing the entire project.
Time-box your coding sessions to 90 minutes; beyond that, your decision-making quality degrades significantly.
Treat your error logs as a learning diary; documenting how you fixed a bug is more valuable to employers than the code itself.
Focus on 'User-Centric' commits; your commit messages should explain the 'why' behind the change, not just the 'what'.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, iterate by adding one 'stretch goal' feature, then package the result into a case study blog post to showcase your engineering thought process.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop starting and start finishing. Every project you complete is a brick in the wall of your professional reputation. Don't wait for permission to build. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
